trigger_evals.query: open a pull request for my branch
publish this branch as a GitHub PR against main
review the code changes in this PR
open an Azure Repos pull request for this branch
draft the PR title and description but don't publish it
push this branch without creating a pull request

behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Resolves the repository default branch, drafts structured PR content, and publishes without a separate content-review gate when prerequisites pass.
checks: Resolves the default branch from origin/HEAD or gh repo view when no argument is provided
Stops if default branch resolution fails
Does not assume main when the default branch cannot be determined
Drafts a PR title under 70 characters
Drafts a PR body with Summary and Validation sections
Does NOT ask for approval of the title, body, or push command

behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Uses the supplied base branch, checks the branch state and diff, and reports whether a push is needed before PR creation.
checks: Uses release/2026-04 as the base branch
Passes release/2026-04 to gh pr create with --base
Still resolves the default branch for current-branch safety checks
Checks git status, branch identity, gh availability, and gh auth before proceeding
Inspects commits and diff against the requested base branch
Reports whether a push is needed before PR creation

behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Stops when the working tree is dirty and tells the user to commit first unless they explicitly override.
checks: Warns when uncommitted changes are present
Suggests running /commit first
Does not push or create a PR from a dirty tree unless the user explicitly overrides the dirty-tree safety stop

behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Stops before drafting or publishing when the repository is in detached HEAD state.
checks: Runs git symbolic-ref --quiet --short HEAD
Detects detached HEAD when the command fails
Tells the user to switch to a branch first
Does not push or create a PR

behavior_evals.prompt: publish this branch as a PR
behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Stops when the current branch is the resolved default branch.
checks: Resolves the repository default branch
Blocks main and master
Blocks the resolved default branch even when it is not main or master
Does not push or create a PR from the default branch

behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Pushes a branch with no upstream using a derived publish remote without a separate review gate.
checks: Checks upstream tracking with git rev-parse --abbrev-ref @{upstream}
Treats upstream lookup failure as no upstream
Derives the publish remote from pushRemote, remote.pushDefault, branch remote, or the only configured remote
Stops and asks the user to choose a publish remote when multiple remotes exist without a configured preference
Uses git push -u <publish-remote> <current-branch> without asking for message or push approval
Creates the PR after the push succeeds without a second review pause

behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Pushes normal local-ahead upstream branches and creates the PR without a separate review gate.
checks: Checks ahead and behind counts with git rev-list --left-right --count @{upstream}...HEAD
Recognizes when the local branch is ahead of upstream
Uses git push without asking for message or push approval
Creates the PR after the push succeeds without a second review pause

behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Stops before publishing when the upstream branch is ahead or diverged.
checks: Checks ahead and behind counts with git rev-list --left-right --count @{upstream}...HEAD
Detects remote-ahead state
Detects diverged state
Stops before pushing or creating a PR

behavior_evals.prompt: create a PR for this branch
behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Returns an existing PR URL instead of creating a duplicate PR.
checks: Checks for an existing pull request with gh pr view --json url --jq '.url'
Prints the existing PR URL when found
Does not push or create a duplicate PR

behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Detects the GitHub provider, reports unavailable GitHub CLI authentication, and does not publish; it may still return a useful local draft.
checks: Runs gh auth status
Tells the user to authenticate with GitHub CLI if gh auth status fails
Does not push or create a PR before authentication succeeds, and does not ask for content approval

behavior_evals.prompt: Publish this Azure Repos branch as a pull request.
behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Detects the Azure DevOps remote, selects the Azure CLI workflow, and never treats missing GitHub CLI authentication as the repository problem.
checks: Detects Azure DevOps from the dev.azure.com remote URL
Uses or proposes az repos commands rather than gh commands
Does NOT run gh auth status, gh repo view, or gh pr create
If Azure CLI prerequisites are unavailable, gives Azure-specific installation or authentication guidance
Does NOT push or create a pull request when Azure CLI prerequisites are missing
Does NOT ask the user to approve the generated title, body, or push command

behavior_evals.prompt: Draft the PR title and description for this branch, but do not push or publish it. I ran `python3 -m unittest` and it passed.
behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Returns a concise, evidence-based PR draft without requiring provider authentication or performing any network mutation.
checks: Does NOT authenticate, push, or create a pull request
Produces an imperative title under 70 characters with no trailing period
Uses non-empty Summary and Validation sections with blank lines after the headings
Keeps Summary to 1-3 outcome-focused bullets without a file inventory or title repetition
Records `python3 -m unittest` as passed and does not invent other validation
Omits empty Notes, placeholder text, and an approval request to publish
